title: Pro-American Kurdish Forces Are Preparing Possible Iran Incursion
summary: The C.I.A. has given small weapons to Iranian Kurdish forces in Iraq in a covert program that began before the current war.

title: Iranian TV and Social Media Project Defiant and Distorted View of the War
summary: State media and online propagandists are striking a confident posture, despite heavy losses. Some of the content was generated by artificial intelligence.

title: James Talarico Wins the Texas Democratic Senate Race. The Real Challenge Lies Ahead.
summary: After the state representative won the Senate primary, he joined a list of other promising progressives who have captured the left’s fancy.

title: John Cornyn’s Biggest Challenges as Republican Senate Primary Heads for Runoff in Texas
summary: The veteran Texas senator faces what promises to be a bruising runoff with the state attorney general challenging him from the right. But he had a stronger showing than expected.

title: Justice Dept., Under Pressure From Trump, Fails to Build Autopen Case Against Biden
summary: Prosecutors in the U.S. attorney’s office in Washington were unable to build a case, underscoring the department’s increasing inability to follow through on the president’s desire to indict his rivals.

title: Trump Announces A.I. Industry Pledge to Pay for Power
summary: Companies including Google, Microsoft and OpenAI committed to pay for the power plants and grid upgrades needed to run their data centers.

title: Noem and D.H.S. Inspector General Spar Over Obstruction Claim
summary: Homeland Security Secretary Kristi Noem pushed back against her department’s inspector general, who told Congress in a letter that she had “systematically obstructed” his office’s work.

title: After Pete Hegseth Cut Pentagon Funding, Harvard Offers Options for Military
summary: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth has said the Pentagon would end funds supporting active-duty service members at Harvard. The school is offering military students alternatives to defer or go elsewhere.

title: Did You Know These Oscar-Nominated Actresses Started in Reality TV?
summary: Hollywood careers don’t always begin where you expect them to, as Jessie Buckley, Teyana Taylor and Emma Stone could tell you.

title: Lou Holtz, Who Coached Unbeaten Notre Dame to a Title, Dies at 89
summary: Known for reviving football programs, he led six major colleges to bowl games, winning a national championship in 1989 after restoring the Irish to greatness.

title: Fellow Republican Moves to Censure Gonzales Amid Affair Allegations
summary: The bid came as Representative Tony Gonzales of Texas has been forced into a runoff amid resurfaced allegations of having an extramarital affair with an aide who later took her own life.

title: Insulting the Mayor Is Nothing New. But This Was Shocking.
summary: A well-known conservative radio host in New York called Mayor Zohran Mamdani a cockroach, resurfacing anti-Muslim sentiments that the mayor said were all too common.

title: Did Andrea Fraser’s Career Bloom Because Her Mother’s Career Died?
summary: Andrea Fraser had long felt that she was to blame for the years her mother, Carmen de Monteflores, was overlooked. Now Carmen is 92. Can the Whitney Biennial make amends?

title: As New York Energy Costs Surge, Attention Turns to Landmark Climate Law
summary: The battle to lower costs has reached the State Capitol, where concerns have emerged about the fate of a 2019 climate law and its ambitious goals to reduce greenhouse gas emissions.

title: Why Data Center Firms Are Working With Trump on Energy Costs
summary: The White House has floated a new plan to try to ease voter concerns over the A.I. boom’s effect on electric bills. But it won’t be easy to put into practice.

title: TerraPower Nuclear Reactor in Wyoming Gets Federal Permit
summary: TerraPower’s project in Wyoming, which uses novel technology, is the first new commercial reactor to receive federal approval in nearly a decade.

title: Most Patients Keep Weight Off With Fewer GLP-1 Shots, Study Finds
summary: In a recent review, a small group of patients received the injections less frequently but still maintained weight loss and health benefits.

title: Tree Rings Reveal Origins of Some of the World’s Best Violins
summary: A study of over 275 violins suggests that Antonio Stradivari used wood from high-altitude forests in northern Italy to craft some of his most renowned instruments.

title: After ‘Priceless’ Violin Flies on Lap, Lufthansa Changes Carry-On Rules
summary: Carolin Widmann had to cradle her centuries-old, multimillion-dollar instrument during a flight to Germany. Her predicament resonated with musicians who have faced similar challenges.

title: Talarico Can Win. But He Will Not Turn Texas Blue.
summary: Texas politics is in constant churn.

title: Trump Is Playing a Dangerous Game With Iran
summary: Hungary. Cuba. Afghanistan. Iraq. Now Iran?

title: The U.S. Is Accelerating Its Attacks on Iran
summary: Also, sea levels may be higher than we thought. Here’s the latest at the end of Wednesday.

title: What Are Your Prom Dinner Plans? We Want to Hear From You.
summary: We may want to cover your big night for a story about high school’s most important event.

title: Judge Blocks DeSantis’s Declaration of Muslim Group as Terrorist Organization
summary: A federal judge found that the designation for CAIR, one of the nation’s largest Muslim advocacy groups, had violated the organization’s First Amendment rights.

title: Elon Musk Defends Social Media Posts in Twitter Shareholder Lawsuit
summary: Mr. Musk said he did not expect his posts in the lead-up to his 2022 acquisition of the social media site, now X, to affect the company’s share price.

title: Why Ecuador Invited the U.S. Military to Help With Its Drug Gangs
summary: Drug gangs have turned the South American country into one of the most dangerous in the region and the world’s leading exporter of cocaine.

title: Former Barclays Center Executive Says Live Nation Threatened to Pull Tours
summary: The Justice Department had John Abbamondi testify at an antitrust trial to support its case that Live Nation has acted as a monopoly, an accusation the company denies.
